paper_title: Planktonic Foraminifera δ 18 O and Mg/Ca Measurements

methods.description: Process for selecting and preparing planktonic foraminifera specimens from sediment samples for subsequent stable isotope and Mg/Ca analysis.

steps.description: Between 40 and 70 specimens of Turborotalita quinqueloba were selected from the 150-212 μm size fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: select specific foraminifera species from a size fraction for stable isotope analysis

steps.description: Around 180-200 individuals of the polar planktonic foraminifera species Neogloboquadrina pachyderma (sinistral) (hereinafter Nps) were picked from the 150-212 μm size fraction for paired Mg/Ca and δ 18 O measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: select specific foraminifera species from a size fraction for paired Mg/Ca and stable isotope analysis

steps.description: A simple experiment was carried out initially to establish the optimal number of Nps individuals required to attain Mg/Ca values that were statistically representative of the Nps population in our samples. We measured Mg/Ca in 20 subsamples of 40 individuals each from the same interval.
steps.searchable_summary: perform experiment to determine optimal number of individuals for Mg/Ca measurement by testing subsamples
inputs: subsamples of varying individual counts (e.g., 40 individuals)

steps.description: conclude that using 160-200 individuals reduces the natural variability in Mg/Ca measurements from a relative standard deviation of 7.2% in 20 individuals to 2.7% ( Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze variability of Mg/Ca measurements across different individual counts to determine optimal number
outputs: optimal number of individuals for analysis

steps.description: All analyses were subsequently run using 150-200 individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: use the determined optimal number of individuals for subsequent analyses
outputs: prepared Nps samples for analysis (150-200 individuals per sample)

methods.description: Measurement of stable oxygen isotope ratios (δ18O) in foraminiferal shells using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Stable isotope measurements on the foraminiferal shells were performed on the Thermo Finnigan MAT 252 mass spectrometer coupled to a Kiel II carbonate preparation device at Cardiff University.
steps.searchable_summary: measure stable oxygen isotopes using mass spectrometry

steps.description: The spectrometer was calibrated through the international standard NBS-19, and all isotopic results are reported as a per mil deviation from the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite scale (‰ VPDB).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate mass spectrometer and report results relative to VPDB standard

steps.description: External reproducibility of carbonate standards was 0.08‰ for δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: assess external reproducibility of δ18O measurements using carbonate standards

methods.description: Measurement of Mg/Ca ratios in foraminiferal shells using ICP-MS, including sample cleaning and quality checks for contamination or dissolution.

steps.description: Samples for paired Mg/Ca and δ 18 O analysis were prepared and cleaned following the protocol outlined by Barker et al. [2003].
steps.searchable_summary: clean foraminifera samples according to established protocol for trace element analysis

steps.description: The samples were analyzed on the high-resolution inductive coupled plasma mass spectrometry at Cardiff University.
steps.searchable_summary: measure Mg/Ca ratios using ICP-MS

steps.description: with an analytical precision for Mg/Ca ratios of better than ±2% relative standard deviation (RSD) and a long-term precision of better than ±2.5% RSD.
steps.searchable_summary: assess analytical and long-term precision of Mg/Ca measurements

steps.description: Average shell weights and comparison of the Mg/Ca record with metals such as Fe, Mn, and Al suggest that secondary effects such as partial dissolution or trace metal contamination have not altered the primary Mg/Ca signal.
steps.searchable_summary: check for secondary effects (dissolution, contamination) by comparing Mg/Ca with trace metal ratios and shell weights

steps.description: The Fe/Mg in the Nps were mostly found to be below the recommended threshold values by Barker et al. [2003] of 0.1 mol/mol and below 0.1 mmol/mol for Al/Ca.
steps.searchable_summary: check Fe/Mg and Al/Ca ratios against established contamination thresholds
inputs: Barker et al. [2003] thresholds (Fe/Mg < 0.1 mol/mol, Al/Ca < 0.1 mmol/mol)

methods.description: Calculation of paleotemperature and paleosalinity from measured foraminiferal δ18O and Mg/Ca ratios using established calibration equations and relationships.

steps.description: Mg/Ca ratios are converted to temperatures using the species-specific calibration equation for Nps obtained from a North Atlantic core-top study by Elderfield and Ganssen [2000].
steps.searchable_summary: convert Mg/Ca ratios to paleotemperature using a species-specific calibration equation
inputs: Elderfield and Ganssen [2000] Nps calibration equation

steps.description: The derived calcification temperatures were used to determine the oxygen isotopic composition of seawater (δ 18 O sw ) by extracting the temperature component from the δ 18 O of the calcite using the paleotemperature equation [Shackleton, 1974].
steps.searchable_summary: calculate seawater delta-O-18 from calcite delta-O-18 and temperature using paleotemperature equation

steps.description: Salinity was estimated following the modern Labrador Sea δ 18 O sw -salinity relationship from LeGrande and Schmidt [2006].
steps.searchable_summary: estimate salinity from seawater delta-O-18 using a regional modern relationship
inputs: LeGrande and Schmidt [2006] Labrador Sea δ18Osw-salinity relationship

steps.description: and it was assumed that this relationship remained the same down core to enable comparison to modern hydrographic data.
steps.searchable_summary: assume modern delta-O-18sw-salinity relationship is constant down core
inputs: LeGrande and Schmidt [2006] Labrador Sea δ18Osw-salinity relationship

methods.description: Calculation of uncertainties for the derived paleotemperature and paleosalinity estimates.

steps.description: For error estimates we used the average error of ±4% RSD from the duplicate measurements of 20 Nps Mg/Ca samples.
steps.searchable_summary: use average error from duplicate Mg/Ca measurements as input for error propagation

steps.description: and a standard error propagation calculation for a quadratic paleotemperature equation including analytical and calibration errors for paleotemperature and δ 18 O sw which are ±0.8°C, ±0.2‰, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: perform standard error propagation for paleotemperature calculation including analytical and calibration errors

steps.description: Following Schmidt [1999] salinity error calculations based on the uncertainties in the relationship between δ 18 O sw and salinity, we estimate an error in salinity of ±0.8 practical salinity unit (psu).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate salinity error based on uncertainty in the delta-O-18sw-salinity relationship
